---
title: "Gateway Chat"
description: "Headless chat flow wired through ChatGatewayElement for application-driven streaming"
---

A reference recipe that connects `ChatGatewayElement`, `HistoryHandlerElement`, and `LLMChatElement` for production-style integration (no Panel UI).

```bash
pyllments recipe run gateway_chat
```

## Wiring

```python
gateway_el.ports.message_output > history_handler_el.ports.message_emit_input
history_handler_el.ports.message_history_output > llm_chat_el.ports.messages_emit_input
llm_chat_el.ports.message_output > gateway_el.ports.assistant_message_input
llm_chat_el.ports.message_output > history_handler_el.ports.messages_input
```

## Configuration

| Field | Default | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `history_token_limit` | `10000` | Tokens kept in rolling history |
| `model_name` | `openai/gpt-4o-mini` | Model passed to `LLMChatElement` |

The LLM element uses `output_mode='stream'` and `generate_content_on_emit=False` so the gateway or your application code can consume token streams.

## Application usage

After loading the recipe module, drive the gateway from async application code:

```python
turn = gateway_el.submit_message("Hello")

async for event in turn.stream():
    if event.type == "token":
        print(event.content_delta, end="", flush=True)

msg = await turn.final_message()
```

See [ChatGatewayElement](../../elements/ChatGatewayElement/index.qmd) and [MessagePayload](../../explanation/MessagePayload.qmd) for full API details.
